hero.subtitle: Discover your ideal career path, diagnose your current position and access the tools you need to grow with purpose.

talentmap.label: Framework
talentmap.title: What is a Talent Map?
talentmap.description: The Talent Map is a framework for understanding the three major paths of professional development and making career decisions with clarity and intention. It's not a personality test — it's a diagnostic tool that helps you identify where you are, where you're going and what you need to develop to get there.
talentmap.benefit1Desc: Clarity on which path to follow, what competencies to develop and how to communicate your value in the market.
talentmap.benefit2Desc: A common language to map your team's talent and have more meaningful career conversations.
talentmap.benefit3Desc: A reference framework for evaluations, development plans and promotion decisions based on real competencies.
talentmap.stat: A framework used by HR leaders across LATAM

paths.label: The three paths
paths.title: What's your path?
paths.subtitle: Three routes of professional development, each with its own strengths, competencies and ways of creating value.

leadership.name: Leadership
leadership.description: Your energy is in people. You find satisfaction in seeing others grow, building high-performance teams and guiding through vision and influence.
signals: You enjoy developing others more than doing the work yourself
You feel energized after difficult conversations with your team
You naturally think in terms of team and shared vision
Your greatest achievement is the people you've helped grow
Politics and stakeholders don't scare you — you navigate them

pm.name: Project Manager
pm.description: Your energy is in execution. You turn vision into concrete plans, coordinate multiple moving parts and feel satisfied when things actually happen.
signals: You feel uncomfortable when there's no clear plan or delivery date
You naturally map tasks, dependencies and risks in your mind
You enjoy coordinating people with different objectives toward a common goal
You get frustrated when projects derail without anyone to get them back on track
Organization and structure give you energy, they don't exhaust you

specialist.name: Specialist
specialist.description: Your energy is in depth. You find satisfaction in mastering an area of knowledge, solving complex problems and being the most reliable reference in your field.
signals: You prefer to understand something thoroughly before giving an opinion or acting
You feel frustrated when you have to be a generalist without time to go deep
People come to you when they need the most well-founded answer
Your greatest satisfaction is solving a difficult problem others couldn't
Continuous learning is not an obligation — it's a pleasure

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.title: How do they differ?
comparison.subtitle: Each path has a distinct competency profile. Visualize the differences to better understand which one resonates with you.
comparison.note: Paths are not mutually exclusive — you can combine competencies from more than one. The diagnostic shows your dominant profile.

diagnostic.label: Diagnostic
diagnostic.title: Discover your path
diagnostic.subtitle: Two tools to know your current profile and understand what competencies to develop.

quiz.title: Diagnostic quiz
quiz.description: 12 questions to identify your dominant path and your second path.

checklist.title: Competency checklist
checklist.description: Evaluate your current level in each key competency for each path.

talentMapVisual.label: Visual map
talentMapVisual.title: Interactive Talent Map
talentMapVisual.subtitle: Locate yourself on the map according to your current role and identify the distance to each path.
talentMapVisual.note: The map zones represent the typical profile of each path. Your position may be on the border between two paths.

blog.label: Blog
blog.title: Career reflections

about.label: About me
about.title: I'm Susana De Jesús
about.headline: I believe talent needs direction, not just motivation.
about.bio1: I've spent over a decade working with professionals, leaders and HR teams helping them understand their talent and develop it with intention. I've accompanied hundreds of people at career transition moments — and what this work has taught me most is that clarity changes everything.
about.bio2: I was born with a natural curiosity about people: why they do what they do, what motivates them, what holds them back. That curiosity led me to the talent world, and talent led me to create the Intentional Career Path framework — a structured way to understand the three major paths of professional development.
about.bio3: Today I accompany individual professionals who want to grow with purpose, and leaders and HR teams who want to have more meaningful career conversations with their talent.
credentials: +10 years in talent and career development
Experience in sectors: technology, finance, retail and services

philosophy.title: Talent without direction is wasted
philosophy.desc: It's not enough to be good at something. You need to know where you're going and why.

philosophy.title: Clarity is a skill, not a gift
philosophy.desc: You can learn to know yourself better and make more intentional career decisions.

philosophy.title: Development is a process, not a destination
philosophy.desc: It's not about arriving anywhere — it's about growing every day with purpose.
